title: Something For Nothing

jokes.text: Hey, I thought you wanted a rematch.
jokes.setup: Will complaining about how rough the other player was
jokes.punchline: Carlton wanting to play again despite Will's complaints
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Carlton's competitive naivety

jokes.text: Forget it, man. Go pick on somebody your own size.
jokes.punchline: Will telling much smaller Carlton to pick on someone his own size
jokes.explanation: Clever reversal of the phrase given the size difference between Will and Carlton

jokes.text: Say, I've got a half peanut butter and jelly sandwich in my pocket... with your name on it.
jokes.punchline: Pathetic pickup line about sandwich
jokes.explanation: Perfect character establishment - awkward kid pickup lines

jokes.text: Man, those lines kill on the jungle gym.
jokes.punchline: His confidence that it works with younger kids
jokes.explanation: Excellent character writing - shows Ramon's misplaced confidence and age-appropriate targets

jokes.text: It's killing me how much you remind me of myself. Why? 'Cause we're both down with b-ball? Well, no, 'cause don't nobody want to see my frog neither.
jokes.setup: Will identifying with Ramon, implying basketball skills
jokes.punchline: Actually identifying with romantic failure
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection setup and self-aware humor from Will

jokes.text: Hey, man, that's kinda similar to my motto: keep your hands on their thighs.
jokes.setup: Ramon's innocent motto about keeping eyes on the prize
jokes.punchline: Will's sexual version
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay with character-appropriate edginess

jokes.text: Oh, by the way, G, she told me to tell you hello. Really? No, I just wanted to watch the Earl Grey run through your veins.
jokes.setup: Will claiming someone said hello to Geoffrey
jokes.punchline: Cruel joke to see Geoffrey's reaction
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Will's mischievous side and Geoffrey's British stereotype

jokes.text: When we have children like that in England we encourage them to emigrate.
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's dry British response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey delivery - dry, cutting, and very British

jokes.text: Really? I'm feeling kinda lucky, man. Why don't you go outside?
jokes.punchline: Will suggesting Carlton test his luck
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor with Will's characteristic mean streak toward Carlton

jokes.text: Sorry, C., no way to beat it, you're odd.
jokes.setup: Carlton asking about beating the odds
jokes.punchline: Will saying he's 'odd' (weird)
jokes.explanation: Simple pun, functional but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Vivian Banks: any job she wants.
jokes.setup: Discussion of job assignments for Monte Carlo night
jokes.punchline: Hilary showing favoritism to her mother
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Hilary's bias and privilege

jokes.text: It's great to know that all those years of law school are finally coming in handy.
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic comment about his legal education
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware humor about overqualification

jokes.text: I ain't down with that old country club stuff... sitting around all night discussing mayonnaise... with four white guys named Biff. Isn't Biff a riot?
jokes.setup: Will complaining about country club culture
jokes.punchline: Hilary immediately proving his point
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup-payoff with Hilary unknowingly proving Will's stereotype right

jokes.text: I'd rather be stranded at sea with David Duke leading the rescue party.
jokes.setup: Will being forced to go to the country club
jokes.punchline: Extreme comparison to racist situation
jokes.explanation: Bold, topical reference with excellent economy and character voice

jokes.text: How Uncle Phil gonna play a nephew? -How's Daddy gonna play a daughter?
jokes.setup: Will complaining about Uncle Phil's authority
jokes.punchline: Carlton turning it back on Will's situation
jokes.explanation: Decent parallel structure showing family dynamics

jokes.text: I can't believe he thinks I'm too young to gamble. I've been eating Hilary's cooking since I was three.
jokes.setup: Ashley complaining about being too young to gamble
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to surviving Hilary's cooking
jokes.explanation: Good family insult humor with logical structure

jokes.text: It is when your breasts don't come with a receipt.
jokes.setup: Hilary commenting on how to wear a halter
jokes.punchline: Vivian's self-deprecating comment about natural vs fake breasts
jokes.explanation: Sharp, unexpected line from Aunt Viv with great economy

jokes.text: Okay, why is Michael Jackson hanging with that little boy from Home Alone?
jokes.punchline: Will's topical celebrity reference
jokes.explanation: Topical reference that would have landed well in the early 90s

jokes.text: Oh, joy.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil promising to bring her a stuffed Miss Piggy doll
jokes.punchline: Ashley's sarcastic enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective deadpan reaction

jokes.text: Being 13 bites. And I'm too young to know what it bites.
jokes.punchline: Meta-joke about her own innocence
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-humor that's both innocent and self-aware

jokes.text: Why don't you just slap a diaper on me and hang a mobile over my head?
jokes.punchline: Ashley's exaggerated complaint about being treated like a baby
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ashley's frustration with being babied

jokes.text: Shaken, not stirred. I trust you won't be dancing on the table with a lamp shade on your head.
jokes.punchline: James Bond reference combined with party behavior warning
jokes.explanation: Decent Geoffrey moment with pop culture reference

jokes.text: Ante is one vanilla wafer. Table limit, four mint Milanos.
jokes.punchline: Using cookies as poker chips
jokes.explanation: Cute visual gag with age-appropriate gambling

jokes.text: I'm 13! If these were the Middle Ages, I'd already be married... and own a pair of oxen.
jokes.setup: Ashley arguing she's not a kid
jokes.punchline: Historical comparison to justify maturity
jokes.explanation: Creative historical reference that's both funny and shows Ashley's intelligence

jokes.text: I'll get my wallet. -I'll get my Smurf bank.
jokes.punchline: Ashley's childish piggy bank contrast
jokes.explanation: Perfect contrast showing Ashley's contradiction between adult aspirations and child reality

jokes.text: Will, you poor public school casualty.
jokes.punchline: Carlton's elitist dismissal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton line showing his privilege and condescension

jokes.text: Wow, you know, Carlton, you are lucky. That could have been an asteroid.
jokes.punchline: Will referencing Carlton's earlier asteroid statistic
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier scene with physical comedy payoff

jokes.text: We're not here because of the way she shuffles cards, you know what I mean?
jokes.punchline: Sexist implication
jokes.explanation: Cringe humor showing country club sexism, but predictable

jokes.text: All right, Robin from the 'hood is in the house. Give me 7-11, I don't mean the store. Taketh from the rich and giveth unto the poor! Blow on these bones for me, would you, slummy?
jokes.setup: Will starting to gamble at craps
jokes.punchline: Robin Hood reference with street slang
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's street persona in fancy setting

jokes.text: Carlton, and I, too, have a dream. And in it all of the world rises up and overcomes your stupidity... now stay out of my face.
jokes.setup: Carlton trying to give gambling advice
jokes.punchline: Will's MLK reference turned into insult
jokes.explanation: Clever use of famous speech turned into personal insult

jokes.text: All right, my lucky drawers is riding high, put your money on me or kiss it goodbye!
jokes.punchline: Crude reference to underwear as luck charm
jokes.explanation: Typical Will bravado with lowbrow humor

jokes.text: Hoagies, cheese steaks, chicken on a spit... seven come eleven 'cause I'm too legit to quit!
jokes.punchline: Philadelphia food references with MC Hammer reference
jokes.explanation: Good character moment mixing Will's Philadelphia roots with pop culture

jokes.text: Where did you learn? A back alley in Calcutta?
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's shocked reaction with geographic stereotype
jokes.explanation: Good Geoffrey reaction showing his shock at Ashley's skill

jokes.text: Hey! Now, who's hotter than Madonna in a pointy bra?
jokes.punchline: Pop culture comparison for his hotness
jokes.explanation: Typical Will swagger with topical reference

jokes.text: And it is with humongous pleasure that I take this chumpy off your hands.
jokes.setup: Presenter giving Will the check ceremoniously
jokes.punchline: Will's street slang response
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between formal presentation and Will's casual response

jokes.text: Well, we got a club tradition in Philly, too... it says if they don't have one, you don't give them your money.
jokes.setup: Club members expecting Will to donate back the money
jokes.punchline: Will's street wisdom about not giving money away
jokes.explanation: Excellent contrast between wealthy club culture and street smarts

jokes.text: I mean, it ain't like I took the Benz drag racing down Sunset... at 4:00 in the morning. -Tattletale.
jokes.setup: Will defending his right to keep money
jokes.punchline: Accidentally confessing to joyride, Ashley calling him out
jokes.explanation: Good accidental confession setup with sibling dynamic payoff

jokes.text: Whoa, hold up. You calling a red leather suit with matching seat covers a whim?
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil calling Will's spending plans a whim
jokes.punchline: Will's specific, ridiculous purchase plan
jokes.explanation: Perfectly specific absurd purchase that shows Will's priorities

jokes.text: Whoa, puberty's in the house.
jokes.punchline: Will's casual observation about hormones
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective observation delivered in Will's style

jokes.text: How else am I gonna get to basketball camp? -$150. Hey, I gotta make up the differences somewhere.
jokes.setup: Ramon charging 50 cents for boom box repair
jokes.punchline: Massive markup for shoe shine
jokes.explanation: Good economic humor showing Ramon's entrepreneurial desperation

jokes.text: All right, well, hey, let's just get out of here before... Linda Blair's head starts spinning around.
jokes.punchline: Exorcist reference about demonic possession
jokes.explanation: Standard horror movie reference for moodiness

jokes.text: Yeah, but if I take all of you I won't have enough money left over to buy a hat.
jokes.setup: Family asking why they can't all come to brunch
jokes.punchline: Hilary's shallow priority of hat over family
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary moment showing her vapid priorities

jokes.text: But did you know that both of them were wearing nothing but cleats?
jokes.punchline: Specific absurd detail about footwear
jokes.explanation: Specific absurd detail that escalates the gossip

jokes.text: It was the tennis pro in the steam room. But the cleats part is right.
jokes.punchline: Phil correcting details but confirming the absurd part
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing even dignified Phil knows the gossip

jokes.text: It's gold. It's worth something.
jokes.punchline: Desperate attempt to help with implied financial troubles
jokes.explanation: Standard cringe humor from misunderstanding

jokes.text: Oh, sure and Mrs. Silverberg was wearing cleats... because she forgot her shower shoes.
jokes.setup: Phil claiming Will returned the check
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic reference to earlier gossip
jokes.explanation: Decent callback connecting gossip to current disbelief

jokes.text: A few pennies, $94. Why split hairs?
jokes.punchline: Ashley's casual dismissal of huge difference
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery showing Ashley's casual attitude toward money

jokes.text: Like the time I beat Bobby Greenspan at tetherball. After that, he told everyone at school that I took steroids.
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous accusation about steroids in tetherball
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist humor with perfect character voice

jokes.text: I had to stuff Bobby Greenspan in his locker before he would apologize.
jokes.setup: Ashley's story about Bobby's steroid accusation
jokes.punchline: Her violent solution to get an apology
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Ashley's tougher side

jokes.text: And if you beat me again, I'm taking my ball and I ain't being your friend.
jokes.punchline: Childish threat about taking ball and friendship
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ramon's immaturity

jokes.text: You grabbed Geoffrey's underwear by mistake, am I right?
jokes.setup: Will seeing Uncle Phil's angry expression
jokes.punchline: Random absurd guess about underwear mix-up
jokes.explanation: Perfectly random Will guess that's both absurd and character-specific

jokes.text: Wow, Will, you must be loaded! You spent $1,000 and had another $1,000 to give to me for basketball camp? I gotta raise your shoeshine rate.
jokes.punchline: Ramon's entrepreneurial calculation about raising prices
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency showing Ramon's business mindset

jokes.text: Well, you know, it's just a loan, till his sneaker endorsement comes through... you know, around 2010.
jokes.setup: Will explaining giving money to Ramon
jokes.punchline: Absurdly optimistic timeline for kid's endorsement deal
jokes.explanation: Excellent deadpan delivery about impossible timeline

jokes.text: I want to know if you was down with donating... to the United Negro Nephew Fund.
jokes.setup: Phil being generous and offering to donate to charity
jokes.punchline: Will's self-serving fake charity name
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay creating fake charity that benefits Will specifically

jokes.text: You know, they say a Saturday night is a terrible thing to waste.
jokes.setup: Will asking for money for Saturday night
jokes.punchline: Parody of famous anti-drug slogan applied to weekend social life
jokes.explanation: Clever reference - twisting the 'mind is a terrible thing to waste' slogan for party money

tmdb_overview: After winning at the country club's Monte Carlo night fund-raiser, Will learns that he's expected to donate the money to charity.
